pattern: Checking whether i*i's digits can be split into pieces summing to i is a small partition problem, so trying every prefix length recursively decides each candidate cheaply.
hints: For each i from 1 to n, look at the decimal digits of i*i and ask whether some way of cutting them into contiguous pieces makes the pieces add up to i.
Answer that with a small recursion: try every possible length for the first piece, subtract its value from the remaining target, and recurse on the rest of the digits.
Sum i*i over every i that passes the check; i*i has at most seven digits for n <= 1000, so even a fresh recursive check per candidate stays fast.

edge_inputs.description: small n with three qualifying integers
larger n with four qualifying integers

stress.description: random n across the full legal range

complexity.time: O(n * 2^d) where d is the digit count of n*n
